Hey, so I think I'm hearing three different things, but I think one thing that, like, so the three different projects, so the one thing I can think that we can do that might accelerate this would be to just create one single redirect rule that redirects non-dub-dub to dub-dub-dub everywhere.

The second would be doing an audit to make sure that you have consistency across the board.
So I am seeing some issues where you've got linking to trailing slashes versus non-trailing slashes, which will introduce a 301 redirect, which will cause Google to index more pages.
The name of the game here is to give Google the ability to index your site and take advantage of the amount of allocation you have for how many pages it's going to crawl that particular day, right?

So if you keep giving it redirects, if you keep giving it a bunch of stuff that makes it move, you're going to lose the amount of pages that you'll get indexed and the chances of having new content index will be greatly reduced because it's sitting there trying to index, you know, all these different redirects.
So if we create the correct rules, get everything in place, that'll get you closer to getting more stuff indexed, create one single, you know, URL structure and get you there faster.

Once that's done I would have more faith that the new content that you're writing will be perceived correctly and indexed by Google.
